text: What type of organization are you?
Which industry sector best describes your organization?
How many user identities need to be managed?
What is your current IAM maturity level?

options.label: Level 1 – No formal IAM in place

text: What is your expected identity growth rate over 3 years?
Do you have subsidiaries, joint ventures, or M&A activity in scope?
Which authentication methods are required?
Do you need Customer Identity (CIAM)?
What identity lifecycle capabilities do you require?
How important is developer experience (SDKs, APIs, documentation)?

options.label: Critical – developer platform is a core requirement

text: What is the primary identity store / source of truth today?
Do you require machine / workload identities in addition to human identities?

options.label: Yes – cloud workload identities (AWS, Azure, GCP)

text: How important is self-service identity management for end users?

options.label: Not important – IT team handles all identity tasks
Nice to have – would reduce some helpdesk tickets

text: Which access control model(s) does your organization need?
What resources require access governance?
Do you need Identity Governance & Administration (IGA)?
How important are automated access reviews to your organisation?

options.label: Not needed – manual reviews are sufficient

text: How granular must your access policy enforcement be?
Do you need policy simulation or 'what-if' access analysis?

options.label: Yes – mandatory before any policy change

text: How many custom applications require integration with the IAM platform?
What is your current directory infrastructure?
What directory capabilities do you need?
Do you have data residency or sovereignty requirements?

options.label: Yes – data must stay in EU / EEA
Yes – data must stay in India (DPDP)

text: How important is 99.99%+ availability SLA for your directory?
Do you require directory-level audit logging?

options.label: Yes – full audit trail of all directory changes

text: What privileged access capabilities are required?
Who are the primary privileged users in scope?
How critical is privileged session recording for your organisation?

options.label: Mandatory – PCI-DSS / HIPAA / forensic audit requirement

text: Do you require secrets management for DevOps / cloud-native pipelines?

options.label: Yes – cloud provider secrets (AWS Secrets Manager integration)

text: How important is Zero Standing Privileges (ZSP) / just-enough-access?

options.label: Not needed – standing admin accounts acceptable

text: Do you need endpoint privilege management (EPM) for desktops/servers?
Which compliance frameworks must your IAM solution support?
What audit and reporting capabilities do you require?
How frequently are you subject to external audits?
Do you require AI/ML-based anomaly detection for compliance?

options.label: Yes – real-time risk scoring per user

text: What is your preferred deployment model?
Which cloud platforms are you using?
Which systems must your IAM solution integrate with?

options.label: AWS IAM / Azure AD / GCP IAM

text: What is your organization's network architecture?

options.label: Zero trust / BeyondCorp model already adopted
Split – some offices with on-prem, some cloud-only

text: Do you have a SIEM / SOC platform already in place?
What is your container / Kubernetes maturity?
What is your current zero trust maturity?

options.label: Identity-centric zero trust (identity is the perimeter)
Full zero trust across identity, device, and network

text: Do you require device trust / posture assessment as part of access decisions?

options.label: Yes – MDM enrollment required for access

text: How important is continuous access evaluation (CAE)?

options.label: Not needed – periodic re-auth is sufficient
Important – session risk signals should trigger review

text: Do you have an existing threat intelligence or risk platform?

options.label: Yes – Microsoft Defender / Entra ID Protection
No – IAM is our primary risk signal

text: Which AI/ML capabilities are important for your IAM programme?

options.label: AI peer group analysis for access reviews

text: How important is intelligent auto-certification of access reviews?

options.label: Not important – reviewers can manually certify all access
Important – large review volumes make manual review impractical
Critical – AI auto-certification is a core requirement

text: Do you want natural language querying of IAM data?

options.label: Yes – business-friendly access to IAM insights

text: What is your annual IAM budget range?
What is your target go-live timeline?
How important is avoiding vendor lock-in?

options.label: Not concerned – willing to adopt vendor-specific standards
Mild concern – prefer open standards where available
Critical – only open standards (OIDC, SCIM, SAML) acceptable

text: What is your tolerance for a multi-vendor IAM architecture?

options.label: Two vendors acceptable (e.g. IGA + PAM separate)
Multi-vendor – best of breed per domain

text: What level of vendor support is required?
Have you evaluated or shortlisted any vendors already?
placeholder: List any products already evaluated or shortlisted...

text: What were the key failure points or gaps in any previous IAM deployment?
placeholder: Describe any previous IAM failures or lessons learned...

text: Rate each IAM capability on a scale of 1 (low) to 5 (critical):
Do you currently employ remote or hybrid workers?

options.label: Yes, our workforce is fully remote or hybrid.

text: What is your primary approach to managing cloud entitlements across multi-cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GCP)?

options.label: We rely heavily on native CSP tools (e.g., Azure AD Conditional Access, AWS IAM)
We need unified, cross-cloud entitlement visibility, risk scoring, and zero-trust policies.
We are mostly on-premise and looking for a hybrid bridge rather than deep cloud governance.

text: How does your organization currently manage administrative and privileged accounts?

options.label: We use a basic password vault to store privileged credentials.
We dynamically provision Just-In-Time (JIT) access and use session recording for all privileged access.

text: What is your primary strategy for managing third-party and contractor identities?

options.label: We treat them exactly like internal employees (fully integrated).
We use a separate directory and manually manage their access.
We have a dedicated B2B/B2C identity portal with delegated administration.
We don't have a strategy yet; it's currently a major security risk for us.
